summary.en: because, since and as introduce a cause clause inside a complex sentence — they differ in tone and word order, and 'for' is not one of them.
summary.zh: because、since、as 引导原因副词从句，构成复杂句；它们在语气和语序上有别，而 for 并不构成原因从句。

heading.en: What a cause clause is — and why no 'so'
heading.zh: 什么是原因从句——以及为何不能再加 so

body.en: A cause adverbial clause states a reason — 'A because B'. 'because' introduces a subordinate clause, so nothing extra goes in front of it, and the whole thing is a complex sentence. Chinese says '因为…所以…', but English must not pair 'because' with 'so': 'so' is a coordinating conjunction that builds a compound sentence. Use both at once and you mix complex with compound — pick one.
body.zh: 原因副词从句陈述某种原因——“A 因为 B”。because 引导从句，所以从句前什么也不用加，整句构成一个复杂句。中文说“因为…所以…”，但英语不能让 because 和 so 连用：so 是构成复合句的连词。两个都用，就把复杂句和复合句混在一起了——只能选其一。

concepts.sourceRef: because引导从句，所以从句前什么也不用加

heading.en: since and as also mark cause
heading.zh: since 和 as 也表原因

body.en: Besides 'because', 'since' and 'as' can also head a cause clause. Here 'since' is NOT the time-from-a-point sense — it gives a reason ('Since you are hungry…' = because you are). And 'as' is NOT the manner 'in the way that' sense ('eat the carrot as I do') — it too gives a reason ('As you were not here…' = because you weren't).
body.zh: 除了 because，since 和 as 也能引导原因从句。这里的 since 不是“从某时间点开始”那个意思——它介绍某个原因（“Since you are hungry…”即“因为你饿了”）。这里的 as 也不是方式从句里“像…一样”的意思（“eat the carrot as I do”）——它同样表原因（“As you were not here…”即“因为你不在”）。
body.sourceRef: 注意这里的since不是时间副词从句那个从某个时间点开始的意思，而是介绍某个原因

heading.en: Two differences: tone strength and word order
heading.zh: 两大区别：语气强弱与语序

body.en: Tone: 'because' > 'since' > 'as'. 'because' gives a real, solid cause; 'since' cites a fact everyone already knows; 'as' cites something obvious. Word order: a 'because' clause may sit before OR after the main clause, while 'since' and 'as' clauses normally come before the main clause.
body.zh: 语气强弱：because > since > as。because 引述实实在在的因果；since 引述大家都已知道的情况；as 引述显而易见的事实。语序：because 引导的从句可放在主句前或后，而 since 和 as 引导的从句一般只放在主句之前。
body.sourceRef: because引导的从句在主句前后都可以，而since和as引导的从句一般只能放在主句之前

body.en: 'for' can express a kind of cause, but it builds a compound sentence, not a complex one — so what follows it is not a subordinate clause. The two halves are equal in status: the first is a guess, the second adds the reason for that guess. Don't believe the common myth that 'for' simply replaces 'because' with a weaker tone — the usage is genuinely different.
body.zh: for 可以表示一定的因果关系，但它构成的是复合句，而非复杂句——所以它后面的内容不是从句。前后两部分地位同等：前者是个推测，后者补充推测的原因。千万别像很多人以为的那样，认为 for 可以直接替换 because、只是语气弱一点——它们的用法确实不同。
body.sourceRef: for在这里构成复合句（compound sentence），而不是复杂句（complex sentence）

examples.text: The rabbit ate the carrot because he was hungry.

note.en: 'because he was hungry' is the cause clause; it sits after the main clause. A complex sentence.
note.zh: “because he was hungry”是原因从句，位于主句之后。这是一个复杂句。

examples.text: The rabbit was hungry, so he ate the carrot.

note.en: With 'so' it is a compound sentence — fine on its own. But never write 'because… so…' together.
note.zh: 用 so 时这是一个复合句——单独用没问题。但绝不能 because 和 so 连用。

examples.text: Since you are hungry, you can eat the carrot.

note.en: 'Since' here means 'because', not 'from the time that'. The clause comes first.
note.zh: 这里 since 表“因为”，不是“自从”。从句放在前面。

examples.text: As you were not here, we ate the carrot without you.

note.en: 'As' here means 'because', not 'in the way that'. Don't read it as 'just like you weren't here'.
note.zh: 这里 as 表“因为”，不是“像…一样”。别理解成“就像你之前不在这儿一样”。

examples.text: Since all my videos are excellent, you should give this video a thumbs up.

note.en: 'Since' cites something already taken as known — a softer tone than 'because'.
note.zh: since 引述大家都已知道的情况——语气比 because 弱。

examples.text: As I have thick skin, I'm begging you for a thumbs up.

note.en: 'As' cites an obvious fact — the weakest of the three in causal force. The clause leads.
note.zh: as 引述显而易见的事实——三者中因果语气最弱。从句在前。

examples.text: You must really like me, for you have given all my videos a thumbs up.

note.en: 'for' is coordinating: the part after it is NOT a clause. The first half is a guess; the second adds its reason.
note.zh: for 是并列连词：它后面的部分不是从句。前半是推测，后半补充推测的原因。

quizzes.sentence: Because the rabbit was hungry, so he ate the carrot.
quizzes.fix: Because the rabbit was hungry, he ate the carrot.

explain.en: 'because' already makes a complex sentence; English never pairs it with 'so'. Drop the 'so' (or drop 'because' and keep 'so').
explain.zh: because 已经构成复杂句；英语不能再配 so。删掉 so（或删掉 because、保留 so）。

prompt.en: Which marker carries the STRONGEST causal force?
prompt.zh: 哪个引导词的因果语气最强？

explain.en: The scale is because > since > as. 'because' states a real, solid cause; 'as' is the weakest.
explain.zh: 强弱顺序是 because > since > as。because 表实实在在的因果；as 最弱。

prompt.en: Fill in a reason marker so the clause can ONLY come before the main clause (not 'because'): '____ you are hungry, you can eat the carrot.'
prompt.zh: 填入一个一般只能让从句放在主句之前的原因引导词（不要填 because）：“____ you are hungry, you can eat the carrot.”

explain.en: 'since' and 'as' clauses normally sit before the main clause; here either fits and means 'because'.
explain.zh: since 和 as 引导的从句一般放在主句之前；此处两者皆可，意为“因为”。

prompt.en: In 'You must like me, for you gave my videos a thumbs up', what is 'for you gave my videos a thumbs up'?
prompt.zh: 在“You must like me, for you gave my videos a thumbs up”中，“for you gave my videos a thumbs up”是什么？

text.en: An independent clause joined by a coordinating conjunction (compound sentence)
text.zh: 由并列连词连接的独立句（复合句）

explain.en: 'for' is coordinating: it builds a compound sentence, so what follows is NOT a subordinate clause. 'for' is not a true replacement for 'because'.
explain.zh: for 是并列连词：构成复合句，其后内容不是从句。for 并非 because 的真正替代。

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: The rabbit ate the carrot because he was hungry.

note.en: 'because' — solid cause; the clause can also move to the front.
note.zh: because——实实在在的因果；从句也可移到句首。

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: Since you are hungry, you can eat the carrot.

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: As you were not here, we ate the carrot without you.

note.zh: as——显而易见的事实；从句在前。

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: You must really like me, for you have given all my videos a thumbs up.

note.en: 'for' is coordinating — NOT a cause clause. Equal halves: guess + its reason.
note.zh: for 是并列连词——不是原因从句。前后地位同等：推测 + 推测的原因。
